industry_vertical.primary_sector: Education (4/8); Professional Services (1/8); Technology / B2B SaaS (1/8)

business_model.revenue_model: Professional services / project-based consulting fees (1/8); Grant-funded (government) + fee supplement (modest school fees) (1/8); Tuition-based recurring revenue (per-semester enrollment fees) with supplemental ancillary income (1/8)

business_model.moat_and_network_effects: Brand/trust moat + switching costs (relationship-based) (1/8); Geographic catchment moat + religious affiliation loyalty + government funding ties + institutional accreditation barrier (1/8); Multi-layered moat: deep switching costs + procurement lock-in + data scale advantages + community flywheel (1/8)
business_model.key_operational_dependencies: Talent-market-dependent + regulation/aid-funding-dependent (1/8); Government funding (Commonwealth + NSW recurrent grants) + NESA compliance + diocese governance + teacher supply pipeline (1/8); University IT infrastructure + higher education sector health + cloud/hosting infrastructure + Volaris capital allocation (1/8)
business_model.network_effects: Brand/reputation moat + accreditation switching costs + institutional relationship moats (1/8); High switching costs (dominant moat) + moderate data network effects (1/8); multi-layer moat: regulatory accreditation barrier + brand/reputation flywheel + campus network density + government-subsidy dependency lock-in (1/8)
business_model.key_dependencies: Regulation-dependent (CHED licensing) + government subsidy flows (RA 10931/UNIFAST TES) + talent-market-dependent (faculty) + campus infrastructure + partner-dependent (ASU, Coursera, Alibaba Cloud) (1/8); Regulation-adjacent (HE sector funding/demand) + talent-market-dependent + parent-capital-dependent (1/8); CHED accreditation + government tuition subsidies + campus infrastructure + faculty licensure pipeline + Indonesian joint venture structures (1/8)

market_position.target_customer_segment: Government / public sector + multilateral organisations (NGO/international) (1/8); Families with K-12 children in Western Sydney seeking Catholic faith-based education — primarily middle-income households in growing suburban corridors (Blacktown, Penrith, Camden, Hills District, Hawkesbury, Blue Mountains) (1/8); Enterprise and mid-market — higher education institutions (universities, colleges) and private PBSA operators (1/8)
market_position.competitive_stance: Niche specialist (defensible segment) (1/8); Market leader (dominant share in Catholic education in Western Sydney) (1/8); Market leader in STEM private HE (Philippines); niche specialist with rollup growth strategy (1/8)

market_position.customer_concentration: Moderately concentrated (government contract model, but wider than previously estimated) (1/8); Diversified (no single customer >5%) (1/8); Highly diversified — 74,000 individual student accounts, no material concentration (1/8)
market_position.pricing_position: Premium / mid-market for development sector (1/8); Mid-market / accessible (modest fees, below independent school sector) (1/8); Dual-tier: mid-market premium (Mapúa) + value/affordable (NTC/APEC segment) (1/8)
market_position.customer_segment: Consumer (individual students and families) — bifurcated: premium (Mapúa) and affordable/mass-market (NTC, APEC segment) (1/8); consumer (individual students) — specifically lower-income first-generation college students (1/8); Primary: Sub-Saharan African governments (Ministries of Education) seeking measurable school quality improvement at low per-pupil cost. Secondary: low-income families in West Africa choosing affordable private schools. Tertiary: impact investors and philanthropic funders. (1/8)

tech_maturity.stack_generation: Modern cloud stack (Google Cloud, Angular, TypeScript, AWS, Webpack) powering a real internal engineering function (1/8); Transitional (TechOne and FACES platforms, vendor-managed, cloud-capable; MacBooks/Chromebooks across 90%+ of secondary schools) (1/8); Transitional — Coursera-powered LMS with AI capabilities (plagiarism detection, auto-grading), expanding from 15K to 45K students; mix of custom in-house apps, Moodle, Blackboard Learn, and modern EdTech integrations (1/8)
tech_maturity.engineering_practices: Small dedicated engineering team (~8-15 core engineers within ~24 IT/Engineering staff); Head of Data & Engineering leads; hiring Data Engineers and Frontend Devs in Budapest (1/8); IT operations team (~15-25 staff) led by Head of Technology Services (Jackson Chan, ex-Qantas/Vodafone); ICT traineeships active; not a software engineering organisation (1/8); Minimal to basic — no CTO at holding company level; Director of IT at campus level (Joselito Sol Cruz); outsourced-first approach via Coursera partnership (1/8)
tech_maturity.data_infrastructure: Structured data and analytics capability (client-facing BI/ETL on AWS/Google Cloud); ETL pipelines, Tableau dashboards; internal tooling lightweight (1/8); Basic-to-intermediate analytics (BI dashboards + student progress tracking; not yet ML-ready at scale) (1/8); ERP-native reporting with centralized SIS and Coursera analytics; basic to intermediate (1/8)
tech_maturity.security_posture: Basic (no SOC2/ISO27001 found; standard for professional services at this scale) (1/8); Intermediate (institutional compliance baseline expected for government-funded education) (1/8); Basic to intermediate (assumed — regulated Philippine HEI with student data obligations under Data Privacy Act) (1/8)
